avalancheActivity.highlights: The more recent wind slabs can still be released above the tree line.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The sometimes strong wind has transported the fresh and old snow. The wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant at intermediate and high altitudes. This applies in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm sign.
Backcountry touring calls for defensive route selection.
As a consequence of falling temperatures, the natural avalanche activity has already appreciably decreased.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Up to 60 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Saturday. In some localities up to 10 cm of snow, but less in some localities, fell yesterday above approximately 1500 m. The easterly wind has transported the fresh and old snow.
The various wind slabs have bonded with the old snowpack.
Intermediate and high altitudes: Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches and loose snow avalanches are the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Little snow fell yesterday. 2 to 10 cm of snow, but less in some localities, fell yesterday above approximately 900 m. In particular on steep slopes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large slab avalanches are possible as a consequence of new snow and strong wind.
The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs can be released even by a single winter sport participant at intermediate and high altitudes.
Ski touring and other off-piste activities, including snowshoe hiking, call for careful route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Up to 70 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Saturday.
The easterly wind has transported the fresh and old snow. The various wind slabs have bonded with the old snowpack.
Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind slabs can be released even by a single winter sport participant in all aspects at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions. This applies in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. Backcountry touring calls for defensive route selection.
As a consequence of falling temperatures, the natural activity of avalanches will appreciably decrease.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 100 to 150 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Saturday. The moderate wind has transported the new snow significantly.
The new snow has settled to a large extent at low and intermediate altitudes.
Intermediate and high altitudes: Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes.
The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day. This applies in particular on sunny slopes, as well as at low and intermediate altitudes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The sometimes strong wind has transported some snow. The large wind slabs can be released even by a single winter sport participant in all aspects above approximately 2200 m. This applies in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. The current avalanche situation calls for careful route selection.
As a consequence of falling temperatures, the natural activity of avalanches will appreciably decrease.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 100 to 150 cm of snow, and up to 180 cm in some localities, has fallen since Saturday. The moderate wind has transported the new snow significantly.
The new snow has settled to a large extent at low and intermediate altitudes.
Intermediate and high altitudes: The new snow of the weekend is poorly bonded with the old snowpack in many places.
Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes.
The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day. In the event of solar radiation this applies in particular at low and intermediate altitudes as well as.
